license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.z6zf9f6
title: UV Crosslinking of Adherent Cells for eCLIP
author: Eric Van Nostrand

steps_raw.instruction: Aspirate spent media
Place the tissue culture plate on leveled ice or a cooling block pre-chilled to 4°C
Place the above (plate plus ice or cooling block) into the UV cross-linker. Note: Ensure the plate is leveled. Remove tissue culture plate lid for cross-linking.
Transfer the cells to a 50 mL conical tube
Wash the plate gently with 1x DPBS at room temperature (15 mL for a 15 cm plate).
Add enough 1x DPBS to just cover the plate (5 mL for a 15 cm plate)
Aspirate media

Note: If all plates are at equal cell density, one plate can be sacrificed for counting – this plate would be dissociated (with trypsin, accutase, or equivalent) and cell number (per plate) counted at this stage. This is recommended for cell types that require chemical dissociation enzymes to dissociate and be properly counted, as this is not recommended post-crosslinking.
Wash plate once with 10mL of 1x DPBS and add to the same 50 mL tube.
While keeping the cells on ice, use a cell scraper (Corning, CLS3010-10EA) to scrape the plate.
Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an energy setting of 400 mJoules/cm 2 Note: this is a setting of 4000 on many cross-linkers which display values in 0.1 mJoules/cm 2
Gently resuspend until the sample is homogeneous

Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer) Note : ensure cells are re-suspended well before counting Note : for cells that do not easily dissociate into single cells, a separate plate of cells can be counted instead (see * above)
Resuspend in the desired amount for flash freezing Typically 20×10 6 cells per mL.
Aspirate and discard supernatant.
Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.
After frozen (at least thirty seconds), remove from the liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C.
Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by submerging the epi-tubes completely in liquid nitrogen.
Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.
Transfer desired amount into 1.5 mL Eppendorf Safe-Lock Tubes (or equivalent) Typically 1mL of 20×10 6 cells per mL.
